preamble: Raising of the open-mid front vowel [ε] to the close-mid [e] is a frequent sound change, often occurring in stressed syllables or as part of a vowel shift.

examples.note: ATR vowel harmony: [-ATR] /ε/ alternates with [+ATR] /e/ in suffixes when attached to [+ATR] roots in Akan.
Historical raising in Attic and later Greek dialects; eta [εː] merged with iota-direction sounds.
First stage of the GVS: low open-mid [εː] raised to close-mid [eː], eventually diphthongizing.
